entities.name: SORGHUM SHOOT FLY
entities.description: Atherigona soccata, an important insect pest that attacks sorghum

entities.name: HOST PLANT RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of the sorghum plant to resist attack by the shoot fly

entities.name: BIOCHEMICAL MECHANISMS OF RESISTANCE
entities.description: The chemical processes within the plant that contribute to its ability to resist pests

entities.name: IS 2312
entities.description: A sorghum genotype that exhibits resistance to the shoot fly

entities.name: SFCR 125
entities.description: A sorghum genotype that exhibits resistance to the shoot fly

entities.name: SFCR 151
entities.description: A sorghum genotype that exhibits resistance to the shoot fly

entities.name: ICSV 700
entities.description: A sorghum genotype that exhibits resistance to the shoot fly

entities.name: IS 18551
entities.description: A sorghum genotype that exhibits resistance to the shoot fly

entities.name: ANTIXENOSIS
entities.description: A component of resistance where the plant is non-attractive to the pest

entities.name: ANTIBIOSIS
entities.description: A component of resistance that adversely affects the biology of the pest

entities.name: TOLERANCE
entities.description: A component of resistance where the plant can withstand pest damage

entities.name: LEAF SURFACE WETNESS
entities.description: A physical characteristic of the leaf associated with susceptibility to shoot fly

entities.name: LEAF GLOSSINESS
entities.description: A physical characteristic of the leaf associated with resistance to shoot fly

entities.name: TRICHOME DENSITY
entities.description: The density of hairs on the plant surface associated with resistance to shoot fly

entities.name: SEEDLING VIGOR
entities.description: The strength and growth rate of seedlings associated with susceptibility to shoot fly

entities.name: PLUMULE PIGMENTATION
entities.description: The coloration of the plumule associated with resistance to shoot fly

entities.name: LEAF SHEATH PIGMENTATION
entities.description: The coloration of the leaf sheath associated with resistance to shoot fly

entities.name: DAYS TO 50% FLOWERING
entities.description: The time taken for half the plants to flower, varying among genotypes

entities.name: PLANT HEIGHT
entities.description: The vertical growth of the plant, where taller plants showed resistance

entities.name: TRANSPLANTING
entities.description: The process of moving seedlings to the field, which reduced shoot fly damage

entities.name: CLIPPING
entities.description: The process of cutting seedlings, which reduced shoot fly damage

entities.name: P-HYDROXY BENZOIC ACID
entities.description: A chemical compound (PHBA) that increased egg laying by shoot fly females

entities.name: P-HYDROXY BENZALDEHYDE
entities.description: A chemical compound (PHB) tested for its effect on shoot fly damage

entities.name: CU2SO4
entities.description: Copper sulfate, tested for its effect on shoot fly damage

entities.name: KI
entities.description: Potassium iodide, tested for its effect on shoot fly damage

entities.name: 2, 4- D
entities.description: A chemical compound tested for its effect on shoot fly damage

entities.name: EGG LAYING
entities.description: The process of shoot fly females depositing eggs on the host plant

entities.name: SOLUBLE SUGARS
entities.description: Biochemical components associated with susceptibility to shoot fly

entities.name: FATS
entities.description: Biochemical components associated with susceptibility and direct effect on deadhearts

entities.name: TANNIN
entities.description: Biochemical components associated with resistance to shoot fly

entities.name: MG
entities.description: Magnesium content associated with resistance to shoot fly

entities.name: ZN
entities.description: Zinc content associated with resistance to shoot fly

entities.name: DEADHEARTS
entities.description: The symptom of shoot fly damage characterized by the death of the central leaf
